feedback.text: First of all, thank you for giving us, the consumer, the chance to give our feedback/thoughts about the issue. Personally I would kindly ask for protection for past, present and future released games, as some are still played daily to this day and may suffer still if only recent/future games are protected. Back then when a product was bought you owed said product and can still play it given that they were not depended on the internet. Letting a company decide for how long one can play a game they pay 60-90 for can lead to anti-consumer tactics (meaning a company could charge extra or monthly for the mere sake of keeping a single-player game alive), or resulting in the loss of already paid money without any gain. I do not wish to rent a game for said price for an uncertain amount of time, it would only result in extra waste from the CD and the case getting thrown away as they can not be used (legally) anymore. If a product is bought, it is my own and I wish for a future in which I do not have to fear it can be taken away from me because a company thinks they don't need it anymore. I do wish you decide for the best of interest for the consumer and help against the creation of extra waste resulting from companies shutting a game down completely, even though it is only for single player and therefore would not need an extra server/ internet connection.
